title: ESCALATE: Status code mismatch — server sends session_key_expired, renderer checks for expired

text: {"type":"escalate","finding":"Status code mismatch","severity":"Critical","description":"Server scraper (scraper.py:117) maps HTTP 401 to status 'session_key_expired', but renderer (tmux-claude-status:298) checks for 'expired' in its case pattern. Expired session keys don't trigger the red color indicator — a functional regression from the pre-server architecture. Confirmed by WSGI integration test.","options":[{"label":"Option 1: Update renderer case pattern","solution":"Add 'session_key_expired' to the renderer's case pattern at line 298: expired|session_key_expired|blocked|error|rate_limited|key_expired)","pros":"Backward-compatible with old bridge files, no server change needed","cons":"Case pattern grows longer"},{"label":"Option 2: Change server status_map","solution":"Change scraper.py:117 status_map to use 'expired' instead of 'session_key_expired' for HTTP 401, matching DESIGN.md","pros":"Shorter renderer pattern, matches DESIGN.md table which says 'expired'","cons":"Loses descriptive status code, requires updating all server tests that assert 'session_key_expired'"}],"recommendation":"Option 1 — backward-compatible, no test churn, immediate fix","promotion_reason":"Natural FIX promoted to ESCALATE — max fix cycles (3) reached after 4 cycles"}

Acceptance (fix-cycle-5, T1.1 combined with TS-33):
- [ ] Line 298 case pattern includes `session_key_expired` (literal string match in the file)
- [ ] The case pattern on line 298 no longer contains bare `expired` as a standalone alternative
- [ ] Lines 22 and 27 (pidfile reads) pass the path via `sys.argv` instead of string interpolation
- [ ] No occurrence of `open('$pidfile')` or `open("$pidfile")` remains in the file
- [ ] The script remains executable (`test -x scripts/tmux-claude-status` returns 0)
- [ ] `bash -n scripts/tmux-claude-status` exits 0 (no syntax errors)
